nodes.en: Encyclopedias are comprehensive general-knowledge reference works organizing information alphabetically or by subject. They synthesize established knowledge from multiple domains into accessible, cross-referenced entries.
nodes.cs: Encyklopedie jsou komplexní referenční díla obecných znalostí organizující informace abecedně nebo podle předmětu. Syntetizují zavedenou vědomost z více oblastí do přístupných, křížově odkazovaných položek.
nodes.brief:
Encyclopedias are comprehensive general-knowledge reference works organizing information alphabetically or by subject. They synthesize established knowledge from multiple domains into accessible, cross-referenced entries, trading the depth of a specialist source for breadth and a single consistent entry point into any topic.

Within [[09]] Reference & Documentation, encyclopedias sit closest to [[09.02]] Dictionaries — both are alphabetically organized lookup works, but a dictionary defines words while an encyclopedia explains the concepts behind them — and they draw content from every other branch of the taxonomy, since an encyclopedia entry is itself a summary of specialist knowledge held elsewhere, from [[01]] Natural Sciences to [[07]] Practical Skills & Trades. Unlike [[09.10]] Historical Archives, which preserves primary source material largely unedited, an encyclopedia is a secondary, synthesized account.

For a canonical general-purpose reference, see the [Encyclopaedia Britannica](https://www.britannica.com/), and for a large collaboratively maintained encyclopedia, [Wikipedia](https://www.wikipedia.org/).
